entries.definition:
-ي
recorded, or the name of their dog (18:9) أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ وَٱلرَّقِيمِ كَانُوا۟ مِنْ ءَايَٰتِنَا عَجَبًا did you find the Companions in the Cave and ʾal-Raqīm, among all Our other signs, so wondrous?

مَرْقُوم marqūm [pass. part.] inscribed, written (83:9) كِتَٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ a clearly inscribed record.
entries.definition_english:
-ي
recorded, or the name of their dog (18:9) أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ وَٱلرَّقِيمِ كَانُوا۟ مِنْ ءَايَٰتِنَا عَجَبًا did you find the Companions in the Cave and ʾal-Raqīm, among all Our other signs, so wondrous?

مَرْقُوم marqūm [pass. part.] inscribed, written (83:9) كِتَٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ a clearly inscribed record.

entries.definition:
-ي r–q–w/y sand dune, ladder, to climb, to ascend, ascension, to advance; a charm, a spell, incantation. Of this root, four forms occur once each in the Qur’an: تَرْقَى tarqā; يَرْتَقِى yartaqī; رُقِىّ ruqiyy and رَاقٍ rāqī.
تَرْقَى tarqā [imperf. of v. رَقِىَ raqiya, trans. with prep. فِى] to ascend through, to climb (17:93) أَوْ تَرْقَى فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ or you ascend into the sky.

يَرْتَقِى yartaqī [imperf. of v. VIII ٱرْتَقَى ʾirtaqā, intrans.] to gradually and deliberately ascend (38:10) أَمْ لَهُم مُّلْكُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فَلْيَرْتَقُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَسْبَٰبِ or is the kingdom of the heavens and the Earth and all that is between them theirs?, then let them ascend to the knowledge/power [commensurate with that].

رُقِىّ ruqiyy [v. n.; n.] ascending, ascent (17:93) وَلَن نُّؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّىٰ تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا كِتَٰبًا نَّقْرَؤُهُ [even then], we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down on us a book that we would read.

رَاقٍ rāqī [act. part.] one who ascends; one who treats the sick by reciting certain utterances (75:27) وَقِيلَ مَنْ ۜ رَاقٍ and it is said ‘Who is [the/a] faith-healer?’

ٱلتَّرَاقِى ʾal-tarāqī (see ت/ر/ق t–r–q).
